name: Poly(ethylene glycol)
bench_knowledge: High-viscosity non-protein liquids (e.g., pure glycerol, PEG solutions) resist aspiration, causing back-pressure that stalls the piston and under-delivers volume. Wide-bore tips reduce hydraulic resistance. Reverse pipetting removes the dead-air step. A 2–3 s post-aspirate pause lets the meniscus stabilise before withdrawal.

Hygroscopic and deliquescent solids absorb atmospheric water during each weighing event. Mass gain directly dilutes the effective molarity of the solution you prepare. Deliquescent solids (e.g., NaOH pellets) can become partially-dissolved slush within minutes in humid air, further confounding accurate weighing. Desiccator storage and fast weighing are the only controls for this.
